rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss And Strike All Filings By Objector Lawrence Porchia And To Bar Further Appearances
rulings.Rulings: The motion is denied. Although captioned a motion to dismiss or to strike, the statutes referred to in the motion are demurrer statutes. As a demurrer, the motion is untimely. Even if it were construed as some other unspecified timely and procedurally proper motion, it would fail on the merits. Mr. Porchia has standing as a nominated executor of the 2004 will, which he has petitioned to have admitted, and as successor trustee of the 2004 trust. If Mr. Porchia prevails in his will contest and proves that the 2015 testamentary documents were procured by undue influence or that decedent lacked capacity at that time, the 2004 documents would be the operative testamentary instruments. The dispositive terms of the 2015 documents are sufficiently different from the 2004 documents that Mr. Porchia is entitled to pursue admission of the earlier will and object to the later will. So long as neither will has been admitted to probate and the validity of the later documents remains at issue, Mr. Porchia remains an interested person for purposes of these proceedings. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m Wednesday, May 29, 2019.
